title: Penelope
content:
آ آ آ آ So youï؟½ve kem ï؟½yer agen,
آ آ آ آ And one answer wonï؟½t do?
آ آ آ آ Well, of all the derned men
آ آ آ آ That Iï؟½ve struck, it is you.
آ آ آ آ O Sal! ï؟½yerï؟½s that derned fool from Simpsonï؟½s, cavortinï؟½ round ï؟½yer in the dew.

آ آ آ آ Kem in, ef you will.
آ آ آ آ Thar, quit! Take a cheer.
آ آ آ آ Not that; you canï؟½t fill
آ آ آ آ Them theer cushings this year,
آ آ آ آ For that cheer was my old manï؟½s, Joe Simpson, and they donï؟½t make such men about ï؟½yer.

آ آ آ آ He was tall, was my Jack,
آ آ آ آ And as strong as a tree.
آ آ آ آ Tharï؟½s his gun on the rack,
آ آ آ آ Jest you heft it, and see.
آ آ آ آ And you come a courtinï؟½ his widder! Lord! where can that critter, Sal, be!

آ آ آ آ Youï؟½d fill my Jackï؟½s place?
آ آ آ آ And a man of your size,
آ آ آ آ With no baird to his face,
آ آ آ آ Nor a snap to his eyes,
آ آ آ آ And nary Sho! thar! I was foolinï؟½, I was, Joe, for sartain, donï؟½t rise.

آ آ آ آ Sit down. Law! why, sho!
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m as weak as a gal.
آ آ آ آ Sal! Donï؟½t you go, Joe,
آ آ آ آ Or Iï؟½ll faint, sure, I shall.
آ آ آ آ Sit down, anywheer, where you like, Joe, in that cheer, if you choose, Lord! whereï؟½s Sal?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Bret Harte (Francis) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Bret Harte (Francis)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
